offer.what: Omni-channel merchant payment processing: POS systems, payment terminals, surcharging / dual-pricing / cash-discount programs, ACH, mobile payments, gateways, and printing/promotional services
offer.coreOutcome: Eliminate or dramatically reduce credit card processing fees — merchants keep the 3–4% they currently lose to card networks
offer.pricePoint: Custom/variable; no long-term contracts; free terminal hardware program for eligible merchants; net cost largely recouped via compliant surcharging passed to cardholders
offer.commitment: Month-to-month — no long-term contract at signing
leadMagnets: Free Payments Audit (review of current merchant statement)
Free Terminal Program for eligible merchants

audience.icp: Small-to-mid-sized business owner in healthcare (dentists, physicians, chiropractors, optometrists), restaurants (fast casual, full service, food trucks, bars), or retail (boutiques, salons, automotive repair, convenience stores) currently paying 3–4%+ in card processing fees and motivated to eliminate or offset that cost
painPoints: Paying 3–4%+ in card processing fees that silently erode margins
Locked into long-term processor contracts with no exit
Opaque monthly merchant statements that are hard to audit
Outdated or fragmented POS systems that slow operations
Healthcare: administrative billing burden and slow patient payment collection
Restaurants: need for integrated ordering, KDS, and delivery tools in one stack
Fear of customer pushback when surcharges appear on card transactions
segments: Healthcare practitioners (dentists, physicians, chiropractors, optometrists, psychiatrists)
Restaurant operators (fast casual, full service, food trucks, bars & nightclubs)
Retail merchants (boutiques, salons, automotive repair, gas/convenience stores)
Nonprofits and educational institutions (special pricing available)

salesCycle.length: Days to weeks — lead submits contact form or books appointment → free payments audit / demo → proposal showing savings → merchant agreement signing
salesCycle.trial: Free Payments Audit; free demo; Calculate Your Savings calculator; free terminal hardware for eligible merchants
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Booked call or in-person appointment with sales rep → audit of current processing statement → side-by-side savings proposal → merchant agreement (no long-term lock-in)
salesCycle.topObjection: Worry about customer pushback when surcharges appear on card payments; skepticism that 0% processing is real / too good to be true; reluctance to switch processors mid-contract

ahaMoment: Merchant processes their first transaction at 0% net cost and sees the savings on their statement — or completes the free audit/savings calculator and sees the exact annual dollar amount they would keep

retention.recurring: Ongoing month-to-month merchant processing relationship; Paymentum earns on transaction volume so retention = active, growing merchant base
retention.expansion: Additional terminals, multi-location rollout, escalating partner program tiers (affiliate → referral partner → strategic alliance → agent/reseller), add-on printing and promotional services
retention.churnRisk: Competitor undercutting on rates, merchant anxiety over cardholder surcharge complaints, business closure or acquisition, hardware/software reliability issues

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — no evident sequence to get new merchants live, confident, and processing on day one
Lead-Magnet Nurture — free audit and demo leads almost certainly go cold without a structured follow-up track
Trial/Consult Conversion — no known email sequence converting audit/demo attendees to signed merchant agreements
Value Newsletter — no visible email content program educating merchants on surcharging compliance, payment trends, or cost-saving tactics
Renewal & Expansion — no evident upsell or multi-location growth emails for existing merchants
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — no known sequence for cold leads or merchants who went quiet after a demo
toneVoice: Professional yet approachable; confident and ROI-focused; plain-spoken (avoids heavy fintech jargon); empathetic to small-business-owner pressures; leads with concrete dollar savings over abstract feature lists
compliance: Fintech/payments: PCI DSS, Visa/Mastercard surcharging network rules (up to 3% cap on credit, not debit), state-specific surcharging laws (a handful of states restrict or ban surcharging — must be flagged per segment). CAN-SPAM for all email. Paymentum is the processor, not a covered HIPAA entity, but healthcare-vertical messaging should avoid reproducing PHI or making clinical claims.
